services.name: Event Hubs
services.description: Ingests real-time multimodal event streams such as audio, image, and text payloads.

services.name: Azure Functions
services.description: Consumes streaming events, normalizes payloads, and coordinates downstream AI processing.

services.name: App Service
services.description: Hosts the platform API and session orchestration layer for multimodal AI interactions.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.description: Performs text generation and vision-enabled reasoning over user prompts and image context.

services.name: Speech Services
services.description: Provides real-time speech-to-text transcription for streaming audio inputs.

services.name: Translator
services.description: Translates transcripts, prompts, and responses for multilingual user experiences.

services.name: Storage Account
services.description: Stores raw media, transcripts, prompts, and generated multimodal outputs.

services.name: Application Insights
services.description: Captures model latency, throughput, errors, and application telemetry for observability.

connections.label: Consume streamed audio, image, and text events for preprocessing
Persist raw media chunks and normalized event payloads
Submit live audio frames for real-time transcription
Translate interim and final transcripts into target languages
Forward normalized transcripts and media references to session orchestration
Send text prompts and image context for multimodal reasoning
Translate user prompts and AI responses for multilingual delivery
Store session artifacts, transcripts, and generated outputs
Emit model latency, token usage, and failure telemetry

workflow.description: Streaming clients publish multimodal events into the platform intake stream for downstream processing.
The stream processor normalizes incoming payloads and stores raw media and event snapshots for replay and auditing.
Audio segments are sent for low-latency speech recognition to produce live transcripts.
Recognized speech is translated into requested languages before being attached to the active application session.
The application orchestrator submits multilingual text and image context to the multimodal model for reasoning and response generation.
Final transcripts, prompts, and AI outputs are saved, while application and model telemetry is recorded for observability.

metadata.prompt: A multi-modal AI platform with Azure OpenAI for text and vision, Azure AI Speech for real-time transcription, Azure AI Translator for multilingual support, Event Hubs for streaming ingest, and Application Insights for model observability
